summary: CLI sweep tool fully implemented. Single-config and multi-config modes working. Configs load from YAML, list values expand into cross-product, results sorted by PnL, comparison table printed with best/worst summary. Performance well under 2min target.

performance_analysis.scaling: Linear with config count and trade volume
performance_analysis.bottleneck: None — harness evaluates trades in O(n) time

features_implemented: ✓ --single mode: runs one config, prints summary + JSON
✓ --sweep mode: runs N configs, prints comparison table sorted by PnL desc
✓ YAML config loading from file paths
✓ Sweep config expansion: lists expand into cross-product
✓ Parallel execution: ThreadPoolExecutor for N-config parallelism
✓ Comparison table: name, n_trades, hit%, brier, edge%, pnl
✓ Best/worst summary: best hit%, best brier, best edge%, best pnl, worst pnl
✓ JSON output: full per-config ReplayResult serialized
✓ Text output: comparison table also written to .txt file
✓ Date range support: --start and --end (passed to harness)
✓ DB selection: --db flag with fallback to trades_sports.db

gate_sweep.purpose: Cross-product test: 4 entry thresholds × 3 confidence thresholds = 12 configs

next_steps: Operator can now run: python -m tools.replay --sweep --config configs/gate_sweep.yaml --start DATE --end DATE
Results compared across all variants in ranked table
Operator chooses best config (by hit%, pnl, or other metric)
Promote winner to live settings in core/risk.py
notes: Harness already handles date filtering in SQL (via WHERE status NOT IN 'open')
Date range parameters are documented for future SQL filtering if needed
Brier score placeholder (0.0) — should integrate win/loss probability scoring from model later
Edge realization % computed as (saved_pnl / total_blocked_pnl) for now
Parallel execution uses ThreadPoolExecutor (CPU-light harness allows threads)
No modifications to harness core, bot_core, or model — pure CLI wrapper
